name: Surrounding the Sierra de Parapanda — via Illora and Montefrio
name_original: Rodeando la Sierra de Parapanda — por Íllora y Montefrío
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Illora are set within the diverse landscapes at the foothills of the Sierra de Parapanda, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by expansive olive groves, agricultural lands, and orchards on the Vega del Genil, providing a picturesque backdrop. Cyclists can experience both well-paved surfaces and more demanding routes that may include unpaved sections, with distant views of the Sierra Nevada to the east. This area features calcareous geology and holm oak forests, contributing to its scenic charm. Best road cycling routes around Illora The most popular road cycling route is Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Íllora, a 68.0 miles (109.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santa Fe – Castle of Íllora loop from Íllora, a moderate 42.6 miles (68.5 km) path. This route generally features well-paved surfaces and offers scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Surrounding the Sierra de Parapanda — via Illora and Montefrio, a 34.6 miles (55.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Parapanda, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Illora is defined by the Sierra de Parapanda, extensive olive groves, and agricultural lands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Illora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Illora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Illora?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes around Illora, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Illora?
A: The terrain around Illora is diverse, featuring a 'multi-coloured landscape' at the foothills of the Sierra de Parapanda. Many routes offer mostly well-paved surfaces, winding through expansive olive groves, agricultural lands, and orchards on the Vega del Genil. However, some more demanding routes may include unpaved and difficult segments, providing an extra challenge.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Illora offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38240670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Íllora&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult ride spanning over 109 km with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38165923" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Íllora – View of Alhama de Granada loop from Íllora&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 105 km and includes substantial climbing.

Q: What scenic views or natural features can I see while cycling around Illora?
A: Road cycling routes in Illora offer stunning natural vistas. You'll ride through extensive olive groves and agricultural lands, with the majestic Sierra Nevada mountains visible to the east. The Sierra de Parapanda provides excellent views, especially along the road towards Montefrío. The region also features calcareous geology and holm oak forests, adding to the picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore the remains of the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Íllora&lt;/a&gt;, which overlooks the village. Other historical points of interest include various watchtowers like Atalaya de la Mesa and Torre de Tocon, remnants of the region's past. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby, offering another glimpse into the area's heritage.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Illora?
A: The road cycling routes in Illora are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful 'multi-coloured landscape,' and the opportunities to experience both well-paved surfaces and more challenging sections.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for those with good fitness?
A: Absolutely. A popular moderate route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe – Castle of Íllora loop from Íllora&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 68 km long and generally features well-paved surfaces. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of Huétor-Tájar – River loop from Tocón&lt;/a&gt;, a 47 km route with a moderate difficulty.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Illora?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the diverse landscapes and varied terrain suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot in Andalusia, and winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, though the region generally enjoys a mild climate.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Illora?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Illora are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38240670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Íllora&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe – Castle of Íllora loop from Íllora&lt;/a&gt; are both circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the road cycling routes in Illora?
A: The municipality of Illora encompasses several charming villages like Alomartes, Tocón, Escóznar, Obéilar, and Brácana. These villages, along with Illora itself, offer opportunities to find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation, providing convenient stops for refreshments or overnight stays during your cycling adventures.

Q: What is the Sierra de Parapanda and how does it feature in the routes?
A: The Sierra de Parapanda is a prominent mountain range at the base of which Illora is situated. It's a defining natural feature of the region, and several routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983635996/surrounding-the-sierra-de-parapanda-via-illora-and-montefrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surrounding the Sierra de Parapanda — via Illora and Montefrio&lt;/a&gt;, lead through or around it, offering excellent views and varied terrain.
tips: Íllora is a municipality located in the eastern part of the Loja region, and where its castle is characteristic. This one, located on a cliff, means that when you approach the town you see it above the rest of the town.
Road that goes from Íllora to Montefrío and where it crosses the east side of the Parapanda mountain range. Its summit is 1600 meters high.
Section of the old Cañada Real from Almedinilla to Íllora, which connects Venta de Agramaderos with the GR-3410 road. It is also known as Camino de Priego.

Beautiful town where its castle, the Castillo de Montefrío is located at the top, on top of a rock. The rest of the population is located at its feet, white in color and where the Iglesia de la Encarnacion stands out with an immense unusual dome.
